What is Cloud Computing?  
Cloud computing means renting servers, databases, storage, and other services over the internet instead of owning them.  

In simple words:  

"You pay only for what you use. No upfront investment, no maintenance headache."

https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/187179495/486492682-0abd599b-77bf-4a54-9751-9e3446a748e5.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTUiLCJleHAiOjE3NzM2NTA4MTIsIm5iZiI6MTc3MzY1MDUxMiwicGF0aCI6Ii8xODcxNzk0OTUvNDg2NDkyNjgyLTBhYmQ1OTliLTc3YmYtNGE1NC05NzUxLTllMzQ0NmE3NDhlNS5wbmc_.I1W_1MA8UnKXjYFLLg0rp-hZx2zmfyBLDVfM9tVEaZw

Cloud providers: Google Cloud (GCP), Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure

Traditional IT vs Cloud:  
Traditional IT	
Buy servers upfront
Pay for peak capacity
Manual setup
High maintenance

Cloud (GCP)  
Rent what you need
Auto-scale as needed
Automated provisioning
Managed by Google

Why GCP?  
Google-level infrastructure (same as YouTube, Gmail)
Powerful networking and AI services
Easy to use UI + powerful CLI
Cost-effective and beginner-friendly
Used by gaints like META
